sections.heading: New Construction Home Inspection in Amherst, MA
What Amherst Buyers and Owners Need to Know

blocks.html: New construction in Amherst clusters in East Amherst infill and subdivisions toward Pelham. Even with current code, we routinely catch missing fire-blocking, improper bath-fan termination into soffits, undersized AC condensate handling, and grading that violates the 6-inch foundation clearance. Pre-drywall inspections catch framing issues that disappear behind sheetrock forever.

Ken Arnold has been performing new construction home inspection services across the Pioneer Valley since 2004 — including hundreds of Amherst-area properties. Every inspection is performed personally by Ken (MA License #587), follows ASHI standards, and is delivered as a comprehensive photo-documented digital report.
An independent inspection before your warranty closes. Phase, pre-drywall, or final-walk inspections — finding what the builder's punch list misses.
For full scope, methodology, and pricing details, see the canonical service page: New Construction Home Inspection. For broader context on Amherst's housing stock, see the Amherst town profile.

items.q: Isn't a new home already inspected by the town?
items.a: Town inspectors check building-code compliance at specific milestones — they don't perform the kind of system-by-system evaluation a private home inspector does. A passed final inspection means the home meets minimum code; it doesn't mean every system was installed correctly or that every detail is going to age well.

items.q: What's the best time to inspect a new build?
items.a: Three timing options. (1) A pre-drywall walkthrough — once framing, rough plumbing, electrical, and mechanicals are in but before insulation and drywall — lets you see things that will be hidden forever afterward. (2) A final-walk inspection just before closing catches finish-quality issues. (3) An 11-month inspection — just before the standard one-year builder warranty expires — surfaces anything that has emerged since move-in while the builder is still on the hook.

items.q: Will the builder push back on bringing in a private inspector?
items.a: Most reputable builders welcome it — a clean third-party report is good for them too. If a builder is hostile to independent inspection, that's information worth having before closing. Ken can discuss how to approach builder coordination when you schedule.

items.q: Do you do all three phase inspections?
items.a: Yes. Many buyers choose pre-drywall plus final-walk; others add the 11-month for full warranty coverage. Each inspection is priced per visit. Bundling two or three phases is available — call to discuss what makes sense for your timeline.

items.q: Is radon testing recommended on new construction?
items.a: Yes — and doing it at new construction is actually the best time. Many new-construction basements in the Pioneer Valley test high. A mitigation system installed during construction is dramatically cheaper than a retrofit — and if you test before closing, you can require the builder to install it as a condition of purchase.

items.q: Should I add thermal imaging to a new construction inspection?
items.a: Strongly recommended — particularly at the final-walk or 11-month phase. Modern envelopes depend on continuous insulation and air-sealing to perform, and thermal imaging is the only practical way to verify it. Missed insulation in a wall cavity, a poorly sealed window, a duct in unconditioned space — all show up immediately on infrared and all should be on the builder's punch list rather than yours. Pre-drywall scans can also confirm batt placement before drywall closes the wall.

items.q: What areas do you serve for new construction inspections?
items.a: Ken serves the Pioneer Valley, Connecticut River Valley, and surrounding communities — including Deerfield, Northampton, Hadley, Amherst, Greenfield, Sunderland, Whately, and more. See the service area page for the full list.

cards.title: Buyer Home Inspection in Amherst
cards.body: A thorough, independent inspection of every major system before you close — 22 years of Pioneer Valley experience documented in a detailed photo report.

cards.title: Pre-Listing Home Inspection in Amherst
cards.body: Find out what a buyer's inspector will find — before you list. Get ahead of objections, price the home accurately, and shorten your time on market.

cards.title: Condo & Townhouse Inspection in Amherst
cards.body: Targeted inspection of your unit plus the shared-system review most condo buyers don't get — common to mill conversions, downtown buildings, and townhouse complexes across Western Mass.

cards.title: Multi-Family Home Inspection in Amherst
cards.body: Triple-deckers, two-families, and four-unit investment properties — each unit and shared system documented separately for buyers and investors.

cards.title: Radon Testing in Amherst
cards.body: 48-hour continuous-monitor radon testing in the Pioneer Valley. Add to any home inspection or schedule standalone.

cards.title: Well Water Testing in Amherst
cards.body: Comprehensive pre-purchase well water testing for rural Pioneer Valley properties. Lab analysis through state-certified labs, results in 5–10 business days.

cards.title: Septic System Inspection in Amherst
cards.body: Pre-purchase septic system evaluation for rural Pioneer Valley properties. Coordinated with Massachusetts Title 5 inspectors when transfer requires it.

cards.title: Termite & WDI Inspection in Amherst
cards.body: NPMA-33 wood-destroying insect inspection — required for most VA, FHA, and many investor loans. Carpenter ant, carpenter bee, and powderpost beetle evaluation included.
